access.admin: Can access governance pages for moderation, users, OAuth, bus, and more. Active suspensions block admin mutations but not read-only admin pages/list endpoints.
access.agent: No admin tools are currently provided.

rules.divided-by-responsibility: The admin backend is divided by governance responsibility, not all actions on one page.
rules.moderation-centralized: Comment and description governance are currently centralized on the moderation page.
rules.high-risk-feedback: High-risk admin actions such as delete, suspend, and hide must have clear feedback.
rules.admin-self-protection: Admin user management must reject self-suspension, self-demotion, and removing the final remaining admin.
rules.admin-rest-session-only: Admin REST endpoints require the in-site Better Auth session cookie and do not accept OAuth bearer tokens.
rules.paginated-moderation-lists: Admin comments, descriptions, homeworks, and users use the shared { data, pagination } list envelope and accept page/pageSize with the deprecated limit alias.
rules.suspended-admin-write-gate: Admins with an active suspension may load read-only admin pages and list endpoints, but admin REST mutations and page actions are blocked with the standard suspended 403 response until the suspension is lifted.
rules.suspension-expiration-validation: Suspension expiration accepts omitted, null, or empty input as permanent and rejects invalid non-empty date strings.
rules.single-open-suspension: Each user can have at most one open suspension. Creating a new suspension closes any previous open suspension for that user while retaining history; lifting an open suspension clears the user's open suspension state.
rules.admin-mutation-not-found: PATCH /api/admin/comments/[id], POST /api/admin/suspensions, and PATCH /api/admin/users/[id] return 404 when the target record is not found.
rules.suspension-retention: Suspension history is retained when an admin account is deleted; creator and lifter actor references may be null after account deletion.
rules.responsive-workspace: Admin management pages share a lightweight workspace order of page identity, feedback, summary or filters, then records; desktop tables retain their density while existing compact record views remain the mobile presentation.
rules.bus-summary-density: Admin bus status metrics use one compact responsive summary instead of a stack of separate metric cards so the active version and first timetable record remain reachable near the first mobile viewport.

notes: Returns 404 when the target user is not found.

fields: Empty inventory explanation and create-client action
